title: Audit data paths for cross-tenant leaks
description: A procedure to systematically check every read, write, cache, and job for a tenant scope and to add negative tests.
body: Use when reviewing a multi-tenant codebase or before a release. Do not assume scoping is complete because the happy path works. Steps: enumerate every data-access path including background jobs, exports, and caches; verify each derives tenant from the principal and includes a tenant predicate; add a negative test per path that a foreign tenant is denied; check cache keys and aggregate queries. Validation signals include negative tests passing for every path and no unscoped access remaining. Risk boundary: any unscoped path is a high-severity leak, so block release on it and escalate findings to security.
